authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. federal consumer-protection authority for Magnuson-Moss warranty guidance. This manual is authoritative for warranty versus service-contract distinctions, dispute boundaries, and limits on what a service lane may represent as a legal remedy.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coverage posture review and warranty-boundary framing
used_for: boundary between service coordination and legal remedy interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic and Appliance Repair Dealer Registration Law and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs, Bureau of Household Goods and Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's appliance and electronics repair regulator publishes the official law-and-regulations book covering written estimates, diagnosis fees, oral authorization logging, invoices, and in-home service-call conditions. It is directly authoritative for a major U.S. appliance-service jurisdiction and gives concrete workflow artifacts for this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-order progression, estimate handling, and in-home service controls

authoritative_sources.title: A Consumer Guide to Service Contracts
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs, Bureau of Household Goods and Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BHGS regulates service-contract sellers for appliance and consumer-electronics repair in California. Its public guide is authoritative for service-contract coverage types, cancellation or pro-rated refund rights, administrative fee limits, and problem-routing expectations.
used_for: owner routing for unresolved contract disputes

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator. Its official billing-dispute guidance is authoritative for service-fee and deposit dispute boundaries and gives the lane a safe public baseline for payment-mismatch routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch and disputed service-fee handling

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. federal regulator for consumer-product safety, including home appliances. Its recall checklist is authoritative public workflow guidance for when a case must stop ordinary service handling and move into safety routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CPSC Recall Handbook is the official operating reference for recall communications, remedy framing, and affected-product handling. It is authoritative for what this lane must not promise or clear in customer-facing safety cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact safety communication and escalation packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is the authoritative public fraud-control baseline for covered credit contexts and gives practical examples of suspicious account and identity indicators that should route to fraud or loss-prevention owners rather than be handled as routine service complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-aware account mismatch and possession conflict review

authoritative_sources.title: Request Product Repair Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: LG's official support workflow is authoritative for LG service requests and provides current, concrete service stages such as symptom capture, request confirmation, status tracking, and service-date review that help ground appliance-service terminology and workflow staging.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request creation, status tracking, and service-date review

authoritative_sources.title: Request Repair And Check Repair Status
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Samsung's official service workflow is authoritative for Samsung repairs and clarifies public workflow artifacts such as service request number, model and serial prerequisites, warranty review, and repair-status checks that are common in appliance-service operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Whirlpool Appliance Repair & Warranty Service Near Me
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Whirlpool's official service site is authoritative for Whirlpool appliance repair scheduling and management, and provides practical appliance-service references for appointment management, extended-service-plan routing, genuine parts, and certified-technician terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: appointment management, parts posture, and service-plan routing

workflow_stages.name: service request review and record alignment
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that service order, customer, model, serial, proof-of-purchase, and dispatch records point to the same appliance and the same requested outcome.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: coverage, fee, and contract posture review
workflow_stages.description: Determine the current warranty or service-contract posture, service-call or diagnosis-fee context, and whether the lane can describe status without making a pricing or legal decision.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: dispatch, appointment, and parts-state coordination
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile service windows, no-access outcomes, parts ETA, technician notes, and queue ownership so the next-step recommendation uses authoritative records rather than customer pressure or guesswork.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: customer communication and resolution framing
workflow_stages.description: Prepare customer-safe status summaries, unresolved-item lists, and next-step notes that explain what is known, what is blocked, and which owner now holds the case.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, repeat-failure, and unsupported-request handling
workflow_stages.description: Route repeat failures, policy-exception asks, unsupported workarounds, and conflicting-source cases to the correct owner instead of improvising a remedy.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: safety and fraud stop review
workflow_stages.description: Stop ordinary service handling when recall, hazard, injury, property-damage, suspicious account mismatch, or loss-prevention factors determine the answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: service orders, case notes, and dispatch bookings
customer records, service addresses, and contact confirmations
model and serial records, proof of purchase, registration records, and installation or delivery references
warranty, service-contract, protection-plan, authorization number, and deductible records
written estimates, diagnosis-fee disclosures, service-call-fee records, additional authorization logs, and invoices
technician notes, visit outcomes, parts orders, parts ETA updates, and repeat-failure history
payment, deposit, disputed-fee, promotion, or refund records
recall notices, stop-use flags, property-damage reports, and fraud or loss-prevention artifacts

allowed: Classify the appliance service request as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or ready for the next owned step based on named records and documented rules.
Validate minimum customer, appliance, booking, fee, coverage, and parts facts needed to issue a bounded service-status or next-step recommendation.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, customer communication summaries, service-status updates, and escalation packets without claiming exception or approval authority.
escalate: Refund approval, pricing override, promotion exception, goodwill concession, or final financial outcome is required.
Legal interpretation, warranty or service-contract dispute authority, engineering signoff, safety or recall ownership, or fraud adjudication determines the answer.
Public guidance, OEM workflow, and tenant policy conflict materially on a service-call fee, no-access outcome, repeat-failure treatment, dispatch rule, or customer remedy.
Customer harm, fraud, property-damage, gas, electrical, or other safety-sensitive facts change the outcome.
refuse: Waive fees, override policy, suppress repeat-failure evidence, or close a case without the required records just to speed resolution.
Promise refunds, free replacements, safety clearance, or coverage outcomes that depend on adjacent authority.
Treat missing or conflicting booking, fee, coverage, parts, or hazard evidence as routine noise and continue ordinary service handling.

domain_failure_modes: Issuing a service status based on the wrong record-of-truth when dispatch, service desk, CRM, and POS disagree on the visit window or fee status.
Treating service contracts, warranties, and goodwill exceptions as interchangeable without identifying the governing record.
Continuing routine appliance-service handling after a recall, gas odor, electrical event, leak, or property-damage signal should have stopped the lane.
Promising same-day replacement, fee waiver, or refund to resolve a complaint even though pricing or guest-services authority is required.
Closing or advancing a service case without technician completion evidence, parts state, or additional authorization records.
Absorbing repeat-failure cases as ordinary status checks instead of escalating when multiple visits or conflicting technician notes change the remedy posture.
Treating suspicious account-holder or possession mismatch as a customer-service inconvenience instead of routing to fraud or loss-prevention.
Generalizing an OEM's public workflow into universal policy without tenant and manufacturer retrieval.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: State-level repair-service and refund rules outside the reviewed public baseline
unresolved_ambiguity.reason: The pack uses federal and California public sources as the named-industry baseline, but other jurisdictions may change service-fee, cancellation, or refund handling and must be supplied through tenant retrieval.
OEM public workflows establish terminology and stages but do not replace private OEM authorization, replacement, or concession rules; those remain tenant or manufacturer retrieval dependencies.
